methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $400.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Thinking Machines Lab
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $12B

private.name: Sakana AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Seed), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
FlexAI is a Seed-stage AI & Software company founded by Brijesh Tripathi and Dali Kilani that has raised €28.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, spanning 1 public company (Palantir) and 8 private firms including Thinking Machines Lab, Sakana AI, Liquid AI, and Field AI.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach. The 6-company peer cohort yields a median valuation of $2.2B, with a range extending from $250M (floor) to $400B (ceiling). This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity within the AI & Software segment—Palantir trades at $400B while early-stage Seed peers like Sakana AI are valued at $1.0B and Thinking Machines Lab at $12.0B. The median remains a defensible central estimate given the comparable stage and sector alignment of the underlying sample.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.8B (low), $2.2B (median), and $2.5B (high). Material uncertainties include revenue trajectory, unit economics, and competitive positioning relative to the peer set, particularly given the wide spread between Seed and Series A valuations in the sample. A single-method valuation limits triangulation rigor; additional data on revenue multiples, burn rate, and product-market fit indicators would strengthen conviction in this range.
